hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212), while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target (SSTR3/5) electrostatic complementarity without compromising SSTR2 binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be achieved by optimizing non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt off-target interactions. This iteration confirms that selectivity is the limiting factor, and the next step is to focus on targeted mutations at positions 1/2/5/6/11/12.

proposed_changes.rationale: To target non-pharmacophore positions where off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 are likely occurring, while preserving the pharmacophore FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.
These mutations are designed to break off-target hydrogen bonds and hydrophobic interactions in conserved pockets of SSTR3/SSTR5 without affecting the SSTR2 pharmacophore.
